nuggets.headline: Before the helmets, Daft Punk was a lo-fi indie punk band called Darlin'
nuggets.text: Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo met at secondary school in Paris in 1987, bonding over music and film. At 17, they formed Darlin', a lo-fi indie punk band named after a Beach Boys song. After their single 'Cindy So Loud' received a negative review in *Melody Maker*, which described it as 'daft punk,' the duo loved the phrase and adopted it for their next musical venture, embracing the thriving Parisian rave scene.
nuggets.imageCaption: Daft Punk's iconic robot helmets provide acclaim and anonymity.

nuggets.headline: 'Around the World' repeats its title 144 times, engineered for pure groove
nuggets.text: You're hearing 'Around the World,' a global phenomenon from their debut album *Homework* that dominated dance charts upon its 1997 release. Daft Punk described its creation as 'making a Chic record with a talk box and just playing the bass on the synthesizer'. The track's hypnotic groove, driven by a steady bassline and that iconic robotic vocal, repeats 'around the world' a staggering 144 times on the album version, a masterclass in infectious rhythm.
nuggets.imageCaption: Michel Gondry's video for 'Around the World' features synchronized dancers.

nuggets.headline: If you love this groove, check out Stardust's timeless French house classic
nuggets.text: If 'Around the World' has you moving, you'll absolutely love Stardust. This French house supergroup featured Daft Punk's own Thomas Bangalter, and their hit 'Music Sounds Better with You' shares that irresistible, repetitive vocal hook and funky, dancefloor-ready production that makes Daft Punk so catchy. It's pure, accessible French house with a similar warmth and undeniable rhythm that builds and evolves, perfect for continuing that groove.
nuggets.imageCaption: Stardust, featuring Thomas Bangalter, created a French house classic.

nuggets.headline: Thomas Bangalter's dad was a 70s disco hitmaker who helped Daft Punk sign with Virgin Records.
nuggets.text: That iconic Daft Punk sound, blending disco and house? Part of that might stem from a family legacy. Thomas Bangalter's father, Daniel Vangarde, was a highly successful disco producer and songwriter in the 1970s, responsible for hits like Ottawan's 'D.I.S.C.O.' Not only that, but Vangarde was instrumental in helping the duo navigate their early career, even securing their deal with Virgin Records, guiding them through the music industry maze.
nuggets.imageCaption: Young Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo and Thomas Bangalter, pre-robot personas.

nuggets.headline: Michel Gondry said 'Around the World' used only five instruments to create its magic.
nuggets.text: When you listen to this track, the genius is in its simplicity. Director Michel Gondry, who created the iconic music video for 'Around the World,' once pointed out how brilliant and minimal the composition truly is. He noted that the song was crafted using only five instruments and very few patterns, showcasing Daft Punk's ability to create a massive, hypnotic groove from essential elements, seamlessly blending disco, funk, and house.
nuggets.imageCaption: Vibrant, synchronized dancers in the iconic 'Around the World' music video.

nuggets.headline: If you love this groove, check out Modjo's feel-good French house sound.
nuggets.text: If you're digging the infectious, feel-good groove of 'Around the World,' you've got to check out Modjo. This French house duo, active around the same era, captures a similar energy with their blend of disco, funk, and dance music. They often feature catchy, memorable vocal hooks and driving basslines that will keep you moving. Start with their huge hit 'Lady (Hear Me Tonight)' for an instant connection.
nuggets.imageCaption: French house duo Modjo, known for their dancefloor hits.

nuggets.headline: A Snarky Review Accidentally Gave Daft Punk Their Iconic Name
nuggets.text: Before they became the robot superstars we know, Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo started a lo-fi indie punk band called Darlin'. After one of their singles was panned by Britain's *Melody Maker* as 'daft punk,' the duo enjoyed the insult so much they decided to keep it for their next musical venture.
nuggets.imageCaption: Daft Punk (center) early in their career, pre-helmets, alongside influences.

nuggets.headline: 'Around the World' Was Once Named the Most Repetitive Billboard Hit
nuggets.text: You’re listening to a track that a computer scientist, Colin Morris, identified as the most repetitive among 15,000 Billboard Hot 100 hits in a 2017 analysis. But that repetition is exactly what gives 'Around the World' its magic, serving as a hypnotic baseline while the song subtly evolves and builds its irresistible groove.
nuggets.imageCaption: Synchronized dancers from the iconic "Around the World" music video.

nuggets.headline: For more French house energy, check out Daft Punk's like-minded peers, Justice
nuggets.text: If the pumping, driving energy and French house swagger of 'Around the World' has you hooked, you absolutely need to explore Justice. This fellow Parisian duo shares Daft Punk's knack for creating powerful, often aggressive, electronic beats that still get a dance floor moving with an unmistakable groove.
nuggets.imageCaption: French electronic duo Justice, known for their driving beats.
